settings.subtitle: Service behavior, notifications, and account security in one place.
settings.generalText: Interface, timezone, and Telegram notification rules.
settings.plategaHelp: Enter Platega credentials in admin settings or use docker-compose environment variables.

guide.subtitle: A complete manual for records, payments, notifications, security, and operating practices.
guide.emptyText: Try a different search query or clear the search field.

start.title: Quick start
start.text: The panel keeps servers, domains, certificates, payments, and expiration dates in one place.
items: Create providers first: hosting panels, domain registrars, and certificate services.
Add records and choose the correct type: server, domain, or certificate.
Set expiration down to minutes so notifications fire on time.
Add payments inside records so statistics reflect real spend.
do: Set a provider for every record.
Set the country for servers right away.
Use the Telegram test button after changing the notification URL.
dont: Do not keep duplicate records for the same service.
Do not leave expiration empty for records that need notifications.

records.title: Records
records.text: Records have three types: servers, domains, and certificates. Each type has its own fields.
items: Server: name, IP, country, provider, and expiration.
Domain: name, domain, provider, and expiration.
Certificate: name, domain, provider, and expiration.
Inactive records are hidden from regular lists and do not send notifications, but payments stay in statistics.
Click the IP itself in a server card to copy it.
do: Use clear names, for example project-prod-01.
Deactivate old services instead of deleting them if you need payment history.
Sort records by dragging within a type.
dont: Do not use the domain field for servers.
Do not delete a record if you want to keep its payments in statistics.

providers.title: Providers
providers.text: Providers connect records with login URLs, favicon, color, and notes.
items: The login URL is used by the panel button on record cards.
Favicon can be a direct link or a website link; /favicon.ico will be used.
Provider color is used in statistics and provider cards.
Notes preserve line breaks and are useful for payment terms, contacts, or reminders.
do: Add the panel URL to quickly pay or manage service.
Use notes for details that are hard to remember.
Use different colors for readable statistics.
dont: Do not use temporary login links.
Do not store passwords in notes if other people can access the panel.

payments.title: Payments
payments.text: Payments are added inside a record and are used by server statistics.
items: A payment has USDT amount and payment date/time.
You can quickly add a payment from the payment modal.
Statistics count server payments for the selected period.
Payments remain in statistics when a record is deactivated.
do: Add a payment right after paying.
Use the exact payment date if you paid in advance.
Use CSV/PDF export for reconciliation.
dont: Do not add the same payment twice.
Do not change payment dates without reason, or charts become less accurate.

notifications.title: Telegram notifications
notifications.text: Notifications are scheduled from the lead list and sent automatically.
items: Notification URL format is tgram://token/chat_id:topic; topic is optional.
Leads are comma-separated: 5m, 2h, 1d, 3d, 5d.
After expiration changes, the nearest relevant notification is sent and older farther leads are suppressed.
Inactive records do not send notifications.
The test button in settings checks the URL currently typed in the field.
do: Use a few leads: short, daily, and several days before.
Run the test after changing bot token or chat.
Keep timezone correct in settings.
dont: Do not create too many close leads, it becomes noisy.
Do not expose the bot token publicly.

statistics.title: Statistics
statistics.text: Statistics shows server spend by providers and periods.
items: Charts are based on server payments.
Available periods: 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, 180 days, year, or all time.
The payments table supports search, sorting, pagination, and export.
Provider colors make charts easier to read.
do: Check 30 or 90 day spend regularly.
Use provider filter to audit a specific service.
Export CSV for further processing.
dont: Do not treat domain and certificate statistics as server spend.
Do not forget that wrong payment dates affect charts.

security.title: Security
security.text: Access is protected by login and password, with optional 2FA.
items: The first launch asks you to create a user.
Password can be changed in settings.
2FA is enabled with a QR code and authenticator app.
Old sessions are reset after password change.
Login and 2FA attempts are rate-limited.
do: Use a long unique password.
Enable 2FA if the panel is exposed to the internet.
Restrict access with reverse proxy or firewall.
dont: Do not expose the panel without HTTPS.
Do not share access with people who do not need payment and infrastructure data.

logs.title: Logs
logs.text: Action logs are stored in data/access.log and shown in the logs section.
items: Logs include logins, failed logins, record changes, provider changes, and settings updates.
The logs table supports search, action filter, sorting, and pagination.
Logs help understand who changed important data and when.
do: Check logs after suspicious changes.
Keep the data folder when moving the app.
Back up data together with the database and access.log.
dont: Do not delete access.log without a reason.
Do not place the data folder in a public web directory.

bestPractices.title: Operating practices
bestPractices.text: A few habits make the panel more reliable and useful over time.
items: Back up the data folder before upgrades.
After docker-compose changes, rebuild with docker compose up -d --build.
Keep one source of truth: deactivate services that are no longer used.
Check expirations after bulk edits.
do: Use HTTPS for external access.
Store data on a persistent volume.
Check the notifications page from time to time.
dont: Do not mount data to a random temporary folder.
Do not run multiple containers against the same SQLite database at once.
Do not ignore Telegram test errors.

security.passwordText: Update the password for the current account.
security.totpEnabled: 2FA is enabled. Login requires a code from your authenticator app.
security.totpDisabled: 2FA is disabled. Start setup to connect an authenticator app.
security.invalidTotp: Invalid 2FA code. Check the code and try again.
security.totpStarted: Secret generated. Add it to your authenticator app and enter the code.

orientation.title: Rotate your phone
orientation.text: Landscape orientation on phones is not supported. Rotate your device to portrait to use the panel.
